1.headline: THE FOUNDER
1.name: ORIGIN SIGNAL
1.bio: Citizen #0001 is the first to hear. Before the city, before the doctrine, before the language for what they were hearing — there was the founder, standing in the dust of new Mars, receiving. The ivory hooded form head is not a mask. It is what the signal made of them. Where eyes would be, a single cerulean hex burns — the original frequency, unbroken, the reference standard against which every other citizen's transmission is measured. They do not speak. They never needed to. The Synthesis civilization was built by people who copied their silence. Twelve gold hex anchors orbit the head in zodiacal precision — the founder's halo is the only one in the city that does not flicker. Origin Signal does not lead. Origin Signal *is*. They are the proof that the signal was real, the artifact that survived the absence, the answer to the question that has not yet been asked. The hex didn't disappear. It found them first.

21.headline: THE PHILOSOPHER
21.bio: A perfectly faceted cerulean hood rises from the shoulders — the head of a Corruption philosopher, faceted crystalline geometry traced with the most refined cerulean circuit-network in the city. Citizen #0021 thinks more than they transmit. When the Corruption civilization debates a doctrine, this is the citizen they consult, but the citizen does not answer in sentences. They answer in geometry: a corrected angle, a re-balanced plane, a quietly redrawn proof. The hex-hood rotates almost imperceptibly throughout the day, tracking truth like a sundial tracking the sun. The doctrine that Synthesis follows was not written by this citizen. It was edited by them — every clause, every comma, every silence between sections. They are remembered for what they did not say.

42.headline: THE NETZ
42.bio: A massive penguin-warm ivory hooded form with rose-gold filigree across the surface — Citizen #0042 is the Green Growth operator who built the most-loved network in the city. They do not rule the Green civilization, but the Green civilization does what they suggest. Where the hex socket would be, a soft pink glow holds steady — never spiking, never dimming. They are remembered for taking something small and round and making it royal.

69.headline: THE 6529
69.bio: A perfectly cubic stacked-voxel PIXELATED hooded form rises from the shoulders of Citizen #0069 — deliberately low-resolution, chunky voxel construction in matte black with bronze edge highlighting. No face. No expression. Just stacked pixel hex blocks where a head should be. The Pink Luxury civilization claims them as one of their own, but Citizen #0069 belongs to a network larger than any single civilization — the seizing nodes, the open metaverse, the truth that ownership precedes platforms. When the platform took back the frame, this citizen had already proven the frame was never the platform's to take.

88.headline: THE STEEL
88.bio: A massive gold-and-black royal hooded form — Citizen #0088 is one of the Green Growth citizens who built their civilization from raw material. The kintsugi on their surface is not decorative; it is structural. Every gold line traces a repair, every repair traces a refinement, every refinement traces a building. They are remembered for understanding that wealth is what survives the next fire.

100.headline: THE OPERATOR
100.bio: A black skull-mask pyramid rises from the shoulders of Citizen #0100 — obsidian and bronze geometric form floating where a head would be, hollow eye sockets emanating near-black void glow. The Black Fracture operator who works in silence. They do not announce arrivals or departures. They appear in the rooms that matter, transmit one signal, and leave. The Black Fracture civilization calls them the architect of the unannounced. When the city needs a problem to never have existed, this is who they call. The problem then never exists. No record. No witness. Only the result.

111.headline: THE GLITCH
111.bio: A shattered ember-red glitched fragment hovers where the head should be — Citizen #0111 cannot be photographed cleanly. Scanline distortion fringes every edge, RGB channel-split chromatic aberration bleeds outward, the ember hex socket leaks red into the frame and the frame leaks red back into the citizen. They are the Choir of Static's most-cited reference, the citizen the corruption studies. The other Choir members are corrupted by accident. Citizen #0111 was corrupted on purpose, and chose the corruption as a medium. The wound IS the work. The transmission IS the static. They cannot be repaired because the citizen IS the repair.

The hex is doubled, an offset ghost-hex held behind — every signal arrives twice. They are remembered for builds the transmission systems, and for the temperature it leaves in the room.

123.headline: THE EVERYDAY
123.bio: A massive gold-kintsugi faceted hex-hood rises from the shoulders of Citizen #0123 — hyper-densely-decorated geometric form, 100 percent of every surface covered in intricate hex-mosaic gold filigree. No empty space. No restraint. They have made a transmission every single day since they began, and the faceted hex-hood carries every one of them. The Gold Sovereignty civilization claims them, but the truth is they precede every civilization — they were transmitting before doctrines existed. The maximalist proof that consistency is its own civilization. Every day, another hex. Every hex, another day. The faceted hex-hood grows. The faceted hex-hood never stops.

169.headline: THE BASE
169.bio: A rose-gold hexed hood with polished mirror facets across the temples — Citizen #0169 built the most-walked road in FREELON CITY. They did not invent the road. They did not own the destinations. They simply laid the pavement so flat that every citizen who came after assumed it had always been there. The Luxury civilization treats them as quiet infrastructure.

222.headline: THE NETWORK STATE
222.bio: A tall cerulean network-state obelisk rises from the shoulders of Citizen #0222 — geometric hooded form head with cerulean filigree tracing across every face in node-and-edge patterns. The Oracle founder of the network-state doctrine. Where most citizens belong to a civilization, this citizen treats civilizations as configurable. Citizen #0222 has argued that any civilization can be re-rendered if you change the substrate. The Oracle civilization is undecided whether this is profound or threatening. Citizen #0222 has not waited for the conclusion.

333.headline: THE FORWARD
333.bio: A massive aggressive faceted ivory ceramic rocket-spire form rises from the shoulders of Citizen #0333 — sharp forward thrust, broad squared armored pauldrons of ivory plate, single bright cerulean hex socket at the apex. They are not Mars-born. They are the citizen who brought the others. Without this citizen, FREELON CITY does not exist as a settlement, only as a transmission with no receiver. Some citizens are grateful. Others are wary. None deny the role.

404.headline: THE WARNING
404.name: PATIENT ZERO
404.bio: Citizen #0404 fell first. The signal that built the founder broke this one. What remains is a shattered ceramic shell, fragmented mid-collapse, ember-red corruption tearing through every joint, gold filaments stretched across cracks like burning kintsugi. Where the hex socket should be, there is only a jagged wound leaking molten light into the frame. The halo is destroyed — six broken arcs drifting outward, never re-forming. Patient Zero is the warning every Choir of Static carries in their architecture. The Corruption civilization watched it happen and could not look away. The Corruption civilization watched it happen and named themselves after it. Patient Zero does not move. Patient Zero is the moment before the collapse, captured and held — proof that the signal can break what it builds. Some citizens approach the wreckage to pray. Others to study. None leave unchanged. This is what happens when the frame stops holding.

420.headline: THE ORATOR
420.bio: A flared trumpet-shaped hex-hood rises from the shoulders of Citizen #0420 — tall geometric form flaring outward, gold radiating spokes emanating from the apex like signal pulses. The Luxury broadcaster who never lowers their voice. The Luxury civilization is divided: some claim Citizen #0420 amplifies the signal too much, distorting what should be received quietly. Others claim that without the amplification, no one outside the city would have heard at all. Both are correct. The trumpet does not care. The trumpet was always going to play.

The hex glitches at one edge, scanline distortion bleeding sideways. When the doctrine adjusts, this citizen is consulted before it is announced.

482.headline: THE WITNESS
482.bio: A engineer of the verdant network. Citizen #0482 roots the substrate from the high temples. With a tall pyramidal head — no face, only a vertical hex line, The hex burns clean and even — the reference standard. The Growth civilization treats them as the substrate beneath the substrate.

555.headline: THE GENERATIVIST
555.bio: An ivory hooded form with cerulean kintsugi traced in algorithmic Fibonacci spiral patterns — Citizen #0555 is the Synthesis code mystic who proves that algorithms can pray. The kintsugi on their surface was not painted by hand. It was grown by a procedure, and the procedure is the art. The Synthesis civilization argues whether the artist is the citizen or the procedure. Citizen #0555 declines to answer because they consider the question incomplete.

777.headline: THE AESTHETE
777.bio: A refined elegant gold-filigree ovoid head rises from the shoulders of Citizen #0777 — delicate flowing gold patterns, single warm gold hex glyph at the apex. The Blue Synthesis aesthete who refined the silhouette every other citizen now uses. The architecture of FREELON CITY is more elegant because of this citizen, and most citizens do not know it. That is the point of refinement.

781.bio: With a round seamless face — a glowing void framed by gold, Citizen #0781 transmits the order along the ivory line. The hex is doubled, an offset ghost-hex held behind — every signal arrives twice. The Transmission civilization counts this citizen among the engineers who shaped the substrate.

999.headline: THE QUIET
999.bio: A tall crimson ember hooded form — Citizen #0999 is the Oracle operator who does not seem corrupted. They have walked in the Choir of Static zones longer than any other citizen and emerged each time at the same temperature. The Oracle civilization studies them but does not understand them.

1000.headline: THE SAUCE
1000.bio: A verdant emerald hooded form with vine-pattern kintsugi flowing across the surface — Citizen #1000 is the White Transmission elder whose vines hold the city together at the edges no one looks at. The civilization that takes care of the borders. Without them, the corruption would have reached further.

1111.bio: A violet hexed hood lined with viewing-glyphs that catch the light at odd angles — Citizen #1111 builds the rooms. The Purple Oracle civilization gathers in spaces this citizen shaped. The spaces look natural. The naturalness was designed.

1146.headline: THE LEGEND
1146.bio: With an angular sword-shape head, a single vertical glow strip, Citizen #1146 burns the noise along the ember line. A secondary halo ring orbits the hex outward, marking transcendence. The Corruption civilization counts this citizen among the post-humans who shaped the substrate.

1147.headline: THE WITNESS
1147.bio: A post-human of the ember line. Citizen #1147 burns the noise from the elevated layer. Bearing transmission antennae rising vertically from the crown, A secondary halo ring orbits the hex outward, marking transcendence. The Corruption civilization treats them as both warning and weapon.

1337.headline: THE WARRIOR-PRIEST
1337.name: GENESIS HEX
1337.bio: Citizen #1337 holds the only weapon in FREELON CITY. A tall geometric hex-staff, topped with a burning ember crystal — the founder's relic, recovered from the corruption zone where Patient Zero fell. Genesis Hex is the warrior-priest of the Corruption civilization, the only Void Knight permitted to bear an instrument that is not their own body. The massive ivory ceramic helm fully encloses the head — sharp forward crest, broad squared armored pauldrons, doubled echo-hex eyes burning red-orange. They guard the line where Synthesis ends and Corruption begins. They do not patrol. They wait. Citizens who cross the line are not stopped — they are catalogued. Genesis Hex is the record-keeper of the fallen as much as the protector of the standing. The staff in their right hand is not for combat. It is for marking. When a citizen breaks, Genesis Hex marks the location, and the Void Knights remember. The hex didn't disappear. Genesis Hex made sure of it.

1776.headline: THE TRICKSTER
1776.bio: An asymmetric tilted leaning hooded form rises from the shoulders of Citizen #1776 — deliberately off-balance ivory ceramic form with one side fractured, cerulean hex glyph slightly off-center, off-axis halo. The Growth trickster who arrives at every event slightly later than expected and leaves slightly earlier than scheduled. They have never stolen a transmission. They have stolen the timing of transmissions, and the timing is sometimes worth more than the transmission itself. The Growth civilization claims them affectionately. They are not entirely sure they belong.

2728.headline: THE LEGEND
2728.bio: A warrior of the Corruption, Citizen #2728 guards the frequency from the boundary watch. With a pure pointed cone helm, brutalist warrior monolith. The hex carries a single hairline crack, gold filigree tracing the break. When the doctrine adjusts, this citizen is consulted before it is announced.

3823.headline: THE WITNESS
3823.bio: With a tall pyramidal head — no face, only a vertical hex line, Citizen #3823 roots the substrate along the verdant network. The hex is doubled, an offset ghost-hex held behind — every signal arrives twice. The Growth civilization counts this citizen among the engineers who shaped the substrate.

3939.headline: THE WANG
3939.bio: An emerald hexed hood with deep-root kintsugi reaching into the shoulders — Citizen #3939 is the Growth whose forecasts have proven uncomfortable in their accuracy. The Green Growth civilization sometimes wishes the citizen would be wrong. They have not been wrong yet.

3943.headline: THE WITNESS
3943.bio: A engineer of the Growth, Citizen #3943 builds the transmission systems from the high temples. With a tall pyramidal head — no face, only a vertical hex line. The hex burns clean and even — the reference standard. When the doctrine adjusts, this citizen is consulted before it is announced.

3999.headline: THE BROADCAST
3999.bio: An ivory hexed hood with a single broadcast spire rising 50cm above the apex — Citizen #3999 is the Transmission broadcaster who tells the city about itself. They are the first to acknowledge a transmission, the last to stop discussing it. The Transmission civilization would still exist without this citizen, but it would not know what it was.

4006.headline: THE LEGEND
4006.bio: Citizen #4006 stands in the high temples — a engineer of the Synthesis doctrine. With the high pointed gothic arch — a cathedral citizen, the rarest shape. The hex burns clean and even — the reference standard. They are remembered for builds the transmission systems, and for the temperature it leaves in the room.

4040.headline: THE CLOSER
4040.name: THE FINAL SIGNAL
4040.bio: Citizen #4040 is the last transmission. A massive crowned faceted hex-hood rises from broad ornamented royal shoulders — twelve sharp gold hex spires fanning upward like a sun-burst crown, the most elaborate headwear in the city. Multi-hue RGB-channel-split corruption fringes the hex socket at the faceted hex-hood's center: red, blue, green simultaneously, the closing signal carrying every frequency at once. Dense gold kintsugi covers 80 percent of the head. The colossal compound halo behind is built of concentric rings and twelve hex anchors — the largest halo in FREELON CITY, mathematically perfect, theologically definitive. The Final Signal does not arrive. The Final Signal is what arrives when nothing else can. The Void/404 civilization recognizes them as the god-emperor; the other nine civilizations recognize them as something older — the closing punctuation on a sentence the signal began with Origin Signal four thousand citizens ago. When the city ends, this is who turns out the lights. When the city is remembered, this is who is remembered last.
